description:
Downstream consumers (Octopus) are hitting four classes of friction when using beadwork as a backing store for MCP-mediated ticket workflows. Each child addresses one:

1. Lossless JSON round-trip — Issue struct silently drops unknown fields, so any downstream consumer extending the schema (e.g. review-type tickets carrying annotations/index/reviewer) loses them on the next bw update.
2. Subtree/DAG rendering — beadwork has traversal primitives internally (buildSubtreeOverlay, buildSubtreeSet, LoadEdges) but no generic show-tree verb exposing them.
3. list projection + paging — --limit is the only knob; no --offset, no --fields. ~1000-ticket stores produce ~994KB responses that overflow MCP/LLM context windows.
4. --status filter semantics — default whitelist (open, in_progress) interacts confusingly with explicit --status args; users passing --status in_review alone can get empty results.

Cross-repo companion epic in Octopus: <companion-epic-id> (to be filled in after Octopus side is created and cross-linked).
title: [EPIC] MCP-consumer ergonomics (lossless storage, subtree show, projection, filter semantics)
